prompt: /perspective quick auth setup — we're using passport.js with local strategy and JWT stored in localStorage, wondering if that's still the recommended approach or if there's something better now

assertions.text: No perspective report file was created — the response is inline only (quick mode should not produce file output)
Response contains a summary of the current auth approach (passport.js, local strategy, JWT in localStorage)
Response mentions at least one modern alternative to passport.js (e.g. better-auth, lucia, arctic, Auth.js/NextAuth)
Response flags the security concern of storing JWT in localStorage (XSS vulnerability) and recommends httpOnly cookies or a more secure alternative
Transcript shows the agent consulted live documentation (Context7 or web search) rather than relying solely on training data
Response is concise and conversational — uses bold text at most, not full report-style headers or a multi-section document
